last_excerpt: abandoned — it was functioning. People were parking on dirt floors and it was fine. Nobody mentioned it. The left-handed bolt thing. I think hot water heater anodes are like that too, or maybe I'm making that up. There are a lot of plumbing facts I'm about sixty percent sure of. Cotton shrinks but polyester doesn't. Or polyester shrinks less. Blends do something in between. I own shirts that fit differently after every wash and I've never once adjusted my behavior around that. Same dryer setting every time. Some birds can sleep with one eye open. One hemisphere at a time. I think about dolphins doing this too, which means there's a version of sleep where you're half-conscious and it still counts. Still restful. Half a brain resting while the other half watches for things. The can of tennis balls. Four cans in a whole life is not very many.
last_concepts: the particular way a ratchet allows motion in only one direction
how a hydraulic press multiplies force through fluid pressure
the mechanics of how a sewing machine forms a lockstitch
the Doppler effect on a passing ambulance
